question: Where do problems usually happen?
title: Where do problems usually happen?
summary: Blank lists, disappearing fields, and deploy mismatches share a pattern: code and data fell out of sync.

scenes.headline: Most failures are alignment problems, not mysterious routes.
scenes.body: Start with the database file, the schema, and whether read and write paths agree—before deep debugging.

problems.title: Blank ideas list
problems.summary: An empty list is usually a normal empty database, a failed query, or a template that hides rows incorrectly.

steps.title: Does the database file exist?
steps.text: Confirm the SQLite file is present on the host.

steps.title: Can PHP read it?
steps.text: Check that the PHP user can read the file and its directory.

steps.title: Does the query return records?
steps.text: Inspect a simple count or select against the ideas table.

steps.title: Does the page correctly display those records?
steps.text: Verify the list template’s empty-state branch is not always true, and that selected columns match what the template prints.

problems.title: A field saves, then disappears
problems.summary: When a field vanishes after reload, five pieces must agree.
problems.note: All five must agree. Agents often update the form and forget either the write statement or the read statement.

problems.title: Works locally, fails after deployment
problems.summary: Code was deployed, but production still has the old schema.

steps.title: Code was deployed
steps.text: The new PHP expects columns or behavior that did not exist before.

steps.title: Production database still has the old schema
steps.text: The migration SQL was not applied on the server.

steps.title: Application code and database structure no longer agree
steps.text: Forms fail, pages error, or fields save without appearing. Treat migration application as part of deploy.
